discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape driven by semiconductors (SMH +4.86%, Intel turnaround, AMD +12% sympathy, TSM +5%) following Intel earnings and Google-Anthropic $40B deal. Healthcare weak (XLV -1.32%) with gene-editing names selling off hard; defense (ITA -1.6%) and regional banks (KRE -1.66%) lagging. Clear divergence: tech/AI euphoria vs. defensive/healthcare rotation out.

notable_index_moves: SMH +4.86% on Intel turnaround narrative; chip sector extremely extended (SMH +30% MTD per CNBC)
XLV -1.32% with gene-editing names leading losers (CRSP -10%, BEAM -7.7%)
ITA -1.6% weak despite ongoing Iran situation — possible peace-deal positioning
KRE -1.66% weak on Fed uncertainty around Powell/Warsh transition

discoveries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
discoveries.mechanism: Gene-editing peer selloff (BEAM also -7.7%) suggests sector-specific catalyst — possibly clinical trial readout, safety concern, or reimbursement news for Casgevy. No news in feed explains magnitude.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown from provided feed; look for Casgevy sales update, competitive therapy news, or partner (Vertex) commentary
research_pointers: Check for Casgevy Q1 sales/patient starts disclosure or Vertex commentary
Scan for any pipeline trial readouts or SAE reports in gene-editing space
Review if Regeneron's gene therapy news (Otarmeni) created competitive pricing concern across gene therapy
Check short interest and recent analyst cuts

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Intel Q1 beat + AI turnaround narrative drives SMH +4.86%
Iran peace talks via Kushner/Witkoff to Pakistan; Trump deprioritizing deal

secondary_opportunities.relationship: commodity (coking coal) — oil/energy complex weak on de-escalation

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump complains Supreme Court tariff decision forces $159B refund to companies; frustration over tariff ruling
trump_signals.note: Post is backward-looking grievance, not new policy. Low direct market impact. However, it signals Trump may pursue new tariff structure to replace struck-down authority — watch for follow-up executive orders. Separately, UK digital services tax tariff threat could affect US mega-cap tech (AAPL, GOOGL, META) if escalated, but mega-caps already rallied today so market is discounting it as negotiation.

discovery.no_signals_note: Several mid-cap movers (CRSP, BEAM, SKYW, ATGE) lack clear catalysts in the provided feed — Michael should verify earnings calendars and recent 8-Ks before assuming mispricing. The semi sector is extremely extended (SMH +30% MTD) per CNBC itself flagging 'getting pricey' — chase trades here carry elevated reversal risk.
